pointblank

Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see https://quarto.org.

Running Code

When you click the Render button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

library(tidyverse)
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
✔ ggplot2   3.5.1     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
✔ purrr     1.0.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(duckdb)
Loading required package: DBI
library(pointblank)

drv <- duckdb::duckdb(dbdir="data/duckdb.db")
con <- DBI::dbConnect(drv)

diamonds_db <- tbl(con,"diamonds.db")



al <- action_levels(warn_at = 1)

diamonds_db |> 
    create_informant(
    label = "A very *simple* example.",
    tbl_name = "example_tbl"
  ) |> 
    info_tabular(
    description = "This two-column table is nothing all that
    interesting, but, it's fine for examples on **GitHub**
    `README` pages. Column names are `a` and `b`. ((Cool stuff))"
  ) |> 
    info_columns(
    columns = price,
    info = "This column has an `NA` value. [[Watch out!]]<<color: red;>>"
  ) %>%
   info_columns(
    columns = price,
    info = "Mean value is `{price_mean}`."
  ) |> 
  #   info_snippet(
  #   snippet_name = "price_mean",
  #   fn = ~ . %>% .$price %>% mean(na.rm = TRUE) %>% round(2)
  # ) %>%
   incorporate()
Pointblank Information
A very simple example.
DuckDB example_tblRows 53,940 Columns 10
Table

DESCRIPTION

This two-column table is nothing all that interesting, but, it's fine for examples on **GitHub** `README` pages. Column names are `a` and `b`. Cool stuff
Columns
carat  double → numeric
cut  string → factor
color  string → factor
clarity  string → factor
depth  double → numeric
table  double → numeric
price  integer → integer INFO This column has an `NA` value. Watch out! Mean value is `~SNIPPET MISSING~`.
x  double → numeric
y  double → numeric
z  double → numeric

2024-07-12 20:40:22 CDT < 1 s 2024-07-12 20:40:22 CDT

 
# 
#   create_agent(
#     label = "A very *simple* example.",
#     actions = al
#   ) |> 
  # col_vals_between(
  #   vars(price), 1, 9,
  #   na_pass = TRUE,
  #   actions=warn_on_fail()
  # 
  # ) |> 
  # col_vals_lt(
  #   vars(c), 12,
  #   preconditions = \(x) x |>  dplyr::mutate(c = x + z),
  #   actions=warn_on_fail()
  # ) |> 
  # col_is_numeric(
  #   vars(price, carat,x,y,z)
  #   ,actions=warn_on_fail()
  #   ) |> 
  # interrogate()

You can add options to executable code like this

diamonds_db |> 
  scan_data()

The echo: false option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
